home.metaDesc: Senior Product Engineer. Full lifecycle across multiple shipped products. Published vlm-code-context-mcp — MCP server for AI agents, benchmark-verified. React, Vue, TypeScript, Laravel, Terraform, AWS/Azure.
home.subtitle: Senior Product Engineer
home.description: Full lifecycle — requirements → UX → frontend → backend → infrastructure. Shipped at GALVANY (lead-matching engine, 3-4× faster assignment), DEMOS (civic-tech platform, Vue + OpenLayers), and bewerbermappe.com (press: BILD, Die Zeit, n-tv). Open source: vlm-code-context-mcp on npm.
home.highlightTitle: MCP server for AI coding agents.
home.highlightDesc: 81 tools. Adversarial benchmark across 9 coding tasks → ~44% median token reduction (range 24-91%, tested against raw codebase context). Persistent sprint/retro memory so agents learn from past work.
home.personQuote: Products don't start with code — they start with the business problem. I work the full stretch from stakeholder interview to production deployment, because the handoffs in between are where good products become mediocre ones.
home.servicesDesc: Requirements → UX/UI → shipped product. Stack fits the job: Next.js, Vue/Nuxt, Laravel, Supabase, Vercel, AWS/Azure.

services.metaDesc: Requirements engineering, UX/UI design, full-stack development with React, Vue, Node.js, Laravel, Quarkus, and modern stack consulting.
services.title: OFFER.
services.subtitle: End-to-End Engineering
services.heroDescAfter: , translated into UX/UI, woven into the brand, and built with a modern stack — Next.js, Vue, Nuxt, Node.js, Laravel, Quarkus, Supabase, Vercel — through to deployment.

serviceDetail.interestedDesc: Let's have a no-obligation conversation about how I can strengthen your product team.

requirements-engineering.title: Requirements Engineering
requirements-engineering.subtitle: Understanding Business
requirements-engineering.description: Good frontend doesn't start with code, but with understanding the business. I capture stakeholder requirements, analyze business processes, and translate them into clearly defined, actionable specifications.
problems: Unclear or contradictory requirements from stakeholders
Features are built but don't solve the actual problem
Missing documentation leads to scope creep
Communication gaps between business and development

process.title: Stakeholder Interviews
process.desc: Structured conversations to capture business goals.

process.title: Requirements Analysis
process.desc: User stories, acceptance criteria, and prioritization.

process.title: Feasibility Check
process.desc: Technical assessment and effort estimation.

process.title: Specification
process.desc: Documented requirements as a foundation for design and development.

ux-ui-branding.title: UX/UI & Branding
ux-ui-branding.subtitle: Design as Brand Message
ux-ui-branding.description: UX/UI design is more than pretty screens – it's an integral part of brand identity. I translate business requirements into visual concepts that match the brand and convince users.
problems: Inconsistent brand perception across digital touchpoints
Design without understanding underlying business goals
Disconnect between brand identity and user interface
Missing design systems for scalable products

process.title: Brand Analysis
process.desc: Understanding brand identity and translating it into design principles.

process.title: UX Conception
process.desc: Wireframes and user flows based on requirements.

process.title: UI Design
process.desc: High-fidelity designs in Figma, consistent with the brand.

process.title: Design System
process.desc: Scalable component library as single source of truth.

frontend-development.title: Full-Stack Development
frontend-development.subtitle: Modern Stack
frontend-development.description: Technical implementation with Next.js, React, Vue, Nuxt, Node.js, and TypeScript on the frontend — and Laravel, Quarkus, or FastAPI on the backend. I build performant, SEO-friendly, and maintainable applications – based on previously developed requirements and UX/UI concepts.
problems: Outdated technologies and lack of scalability
Performance issues due to large bundles
No clear architecture for growing projects
Insufficient test coverage and code quality

process.title: Architecture Setup
process.desc: Next.js / Nuxt App Router, component structure, and best practices.

process.title: Component Development
process.desc: Atomic design pattern with TypeScript and Tailwind.

process.title: Backend Integration
process.desc: Node.js, Laravel, Quarkus or Supabase for auth, DB, and APIs.

process.title: Testing & QA
process.desc: Automated tests with Jest, Playwright, and CI/CD.

project-delivery.title: Project Planning & Delivery
project-delivery.subtitle: Start to Deployment
project-delivery.description: I plan and accompany projects holistically – from the first idea through development to go-live. With clear structure, realistic milestones, and modern deployment strategies.
problems: Projects without a clear plan are constantly delayed
No defined process from requirements to deployment
Missing CI/CD pipeline for automated releases
No monitoring after launch

process.title: Project Planning
process.desc: Scope, milestones, and tech stack decisions.

process.title: Sprint Structure
process.desc: Iterative development with clear deliverables.

process.title: CI/CD Setup
process.desc: GitHub Actions, automated tests, and preview deployments.

process.title: Go-Live & Monitoring
process.desc: Vercel deployment, performance monitoring, and iteration.

modern-stack.title: Modern Stack
modern-stack.subtitle: Full-Stack Toolbox
modern-stack.description: I rely on a modern, proven stack: Next.js or Nuxt as framework, Node.js, Laravel or Quarkus as backend, Supabase or PostgreSQL for data, Vercel as platform, and Claude AI as a productivity tool. All connected through CI/CD pipelines.
problems: Outdated infrastructure with high maintenance costs
No automated deployment and testing
Missing backend solution for rapid prototypes
Manual, repetitive development work without AI support

process.title: Stack Assessment
process.desc: Evaluation of current tools and migration options.

process.title: Backend Setup
process.desc: Node.js, Laravel, Quarkus, or Supabase — the right tool for the job.

process.title: Vercel Deployment
process.desc: Preview deployments, edge functions, and analytics.

process.title: AI Integration
process.desc: Claude AI for code reviews, testing, and documentation.

projects.metaDesc: Selected projects: interactive dashboards, project planners, and admin tools built with React, Vue, Next.js, Node.js, and Supabase.
projects.title: DEMOS.
projects.subtitle: Example Demo Projects

dashboard.title: CRM Dashboard Interface
dashboard.desc: A design-driven demo exploring how data-heavy interfaces can feel refined. Animated KPIs, a drag-and-drop sales pipeline, workflow automation, and revenue analytics — all rendered with custom CSS/SVG charts and deliberate micro-interactions.
dashboard.challenge: Enterprise dashboards often sacrifice aesthetics for function. Dense data tables, generic chart libraries, and utilitarian layouts signal 'internal tool' rather than 'product-grade software'.
dashboard.solution: Designed and built a CRM interface from scratch — animated KPI cards, a pipeline Kanban with drag-and-drop, revenue charts in pure CSS/SVG, and workflow automation views. Every element crafted for visual clarity and interaction quality.

mcp.title: Code Context MCP — Interactive Demo
mcp.desc: MCP server that gives AI coding agents structured access to codebases. 81 tools, adversarially benchmarked across 9 tasks (retrieval, analysis, exploration, implementation, debugging, refactoring). Persistent sprint/retro memory.
mcp.challenge: AI coding assistants waste tokens sending irrelevant context. Developers lack structured ways to feed project knowledge into LLMs, leading to hallucinations and wasted compute.
mcp.solution: Built an MCP server backed by SQLite that indexes codebases into structured context. Benchmarked adversarially against raw-codebase context — ~44% median token reduction, 24-91% range across 9 task types. 81 tools covering file search, sprint management, retros, and team workflows.

enterprise.title: Enterprise Workflow Automation
enterprise.desc: Built and shipped a production SaaS platform that automates complex business workflows end-to-end. From stakeholder requirements through architecture decisions to scalable deployment — this is what I do daily as a SaaS developer.

saas.title: SaaS Platform & Brand Identity
saas.desc: Designed, built, and launched a complete SaaS product — auth flows, subscription logic, multi-tenant architecture, and brand identity. I own the full stack from Figma mockups to CI/CD pipelines and production monitoring.

ml.title: ML-Driven Lead Matching
ml.desc: Explored how machine learning can surface the right seller for every incoming lead — trained on historical company data to predict which pairings convert. The model analyzes past deal outcomes, seller strengths, and lead attributes to recommend optimal assignments automatically.

bewerbermappe.title: bewerbermappe.com
bewerbermappe.desc: Co-developed the digital rental application platform that helps tenants in Germany compile verified documents — income proof, credit reports, and identity verification — into a single professional PDF. Featured in BILD, Die Zeit, and n-tv. Shaped the full user journey from landing page to checkout.
checks: Landing Page to Checkout User Journey

arcade.title: Cyberpunk Arcade Shooter
arcade.desc: A neon-drenched arcade shooter built from scratch with Rust and the Bevy game engine, then compiled to WebAssembly for browser play. Runs at 60fps in any modern browser — no plugins, no installs. Mobile touch controls included.
arcade.challenge: Wanted to explore Rust beyond backend services — the ECS architecture of Bevy, GPU-driven rendering, and the discipline of a borrow checker applied to real-time game logic running at 60 frames per second.
arcade.solution: Built a complete arcade shooter with Bevy's entity-component-system, compiled to WASM via wasm-bindgen, and wrapped it in a Next.js page with custom touch controls for mobile. Zero runtime dependencies in the browser.

about.metaDesc: Senior Product Engineer. Multiple roles across civic-tech, rental-tech, and a funded lead-intelligence startup. React, Vue, TypeScript, Laravel, Terraform, AWS/Azure.
about.title: PROFILE.
about.subtitle: Velimir Müller
about.bio1After: working the full stretch from stakeholder interview to production deployment. Frontend: React, Vue, Next.js, Nuxt. Backend: Laravel, Quarkus, FastAPI, Node. Infra: Terraform on AWS/Azure, CI/CD with GitHub Actions.
about.bio2: Beyond using AI in my daily development, I build tooling for it. vlm-code-context-mcp on npm is an MCP server that gives AI coding agents structured access to codebases — 81 tools, ~44% median token reduction on an adversarial 9-task benchmark, persistent sprint/retro memory so agents learn from past work.
about.bio3: Experience across a corporate (Zalando), a mid-sized civic-tech SaaS (DEMOS), and two startups (Housy/bewerbermappe.com, GALVANY). I care about what happens after deployment — what shipped, what changed for users, what the metrics show next sprint.

exp1.title: Senior Software Engineer - Frontend / Product
exp1.desc: Owned product engineering end-to-end — from requirements and Figma designs through full-stack implementation to cloud deployment. Built a lead management platform (Laravel, React, TypeScript, Inertia, Terraform) that reduced assignment time by 3-4x. Built a data-driven scoring engine for lead-seller matching that improved predicted conversion from ~6% to 11%. Configured AWS and Azure environments with Terraform. Full testing pyramid: unit, E2E with Playwright, visual regression.

exp2.title: Software Engineer - Frontend
exp2.desc: Built and maintained Vue.js applications for a civic-tech platform. Designed architecture patterns for Vue 2/3 (Vuex, Pinia), developed interactive maps with OpenLayers, established testing practices (Jest, Vitest), and mentored junior developers with sessions on TDD and refactoring patterns.

exp3.title: Frontend Engineer / Product Engineer
exp3.desc: Co-developed a digital application portfolio platform (bewerbermappe.com) featured in BILD, Die Zeit, and n-tv. Built a complex multi-plugin WordPress architecture with custom theme development. Shaped the user journey from landing page to checkout. Close collaboration within the agency team from concept to launch.

exp4.title: Web Developer
exp4.desc: Creation and maintenance of WordPress instances with WooCommerce webshops – from planning to deployment.

exp5.title: Specialist
exp5.desc: Specialist at Zalando SE in the e-commerce division.

exp6.title: IT-Trainee - Application Development
exp6.desc: Formal training as IT Specialist for Application Development (IHK certified). Included a 10-month internship at Servicezentrum der Berliner Volkshochschulen with Linux infrastructure, IT security, and development work.

exp7.title: Internship Program
exp7.desc: Administered Linux systems with BigBlueButton, applied IT security concepts for self-hosted servers, developed features with Ruby on Rails and React, and managed internal IT infrastructure.

contact.metaDesc: Get in touch for collaboration, consulting, or project inquiries.

contact.subtitle: Get In Touch
contact.intro: Open for product-engineering roles where the brief runs from requirements through UX, frontend (React, Vue, Next.js, Nuxt), backend (Laravel, Node, Quarkus), to Terraform-managed cloud deployment. Contract or full-time.

footer.taglineDesc: From business needs to deployment — full lifecycle ownership.

privacy.metaDesc: Privacy policy and data protection information.
privacy.title: PRIVACY.
privacy.subtitle: Privacy Policy
privacy.s1Text: Controller within the meaning of the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR):
privacy.s2Text:
The protection of your personal data is important to me. Personal data is only processed on this website to the extent necessary and in accordance with the provisions of the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

Personal data is any data that can be used to personally identify you.
privacy.s3Text: When you contact me via the contact form, the information you provide will be processed and stored.
privacy.s3PurposeText: Handling your inquiry and contacting you.
privacy.s3LegalText: Processing is carried out in accordance with Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR (pre-contractual measures), as the inquiry is related to my professional activity.
privacy.s4Text: The data submitted via the contact form is stored in a database provided by Supabase.
privacy.s4Detail: Supabase is used as a data processor. A data processing agreement exists in accordance with Art. 28 GDPR. Data transfer to a third country is based on EU Standard Contractual Clauses in accordance with Art. 46 GDPR.
privacy.s5Text: The data submitted via the contact form will only be stored for as long as is necessary to process the inquiry or as long as statutory retention obligations exist. After that, the data will be deleted.
privacy.s6Text: You have the right at any time to:
s6Rights: Access your stored personal data (Art. 15 GDPR)
privacy.s6Contact: To exercise your rights, an informal notification by email is sufficient.
privacy.s7Text: You have the right to lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority. The responsible authority is usually the data protection authority of your federal state.
privacy.s8Text: For security reasons, this website uses SSL or TLS encryption to protect transmitted data.
